debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
inst✝² : CompleteLattice α
inst✝¹ : DistribLattice α
inst✝ : BoundedOrder α
S D : Set α
R : @refinement α inst✝² inst✝ S
a b : α
hb : b ≤ a
hb' : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a})
I : Set α
hI : I ∈ R
hI' : a ∈ I
⊢ b ≤ ⊥

failed_attempts:
have h₁ : R.bot_not_mem I hI := R.bot_not_mem I hI
  have h₂ : R.carrier_indep I hI := R.carrier_indep I hI
  simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_sdiff]
  <;> aesop

have h₁ : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := by
    exact hb'
  have h₂ : b ≤ a := hb
  have h₃ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
    -- Since a ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}), and b ≤ a, we can derive b ≤ ⊥
    have h₄ : a ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := by
      -- Use the fact that a ∈ I and I ∈ R to show that a is in the union of D ∈ R
      have h₅ : a ∈ ⋃ D ∈ R, D := by
        -- Since a ∈ I and I ∈ R, a is in the union of D ∈ R
        exact ⟨I, hI, hI'⟩
      -- Now, we need to show that a ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a})
      have h₆ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
      have h₇ : a ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := by
        -- Use the property of the supremum and the fact that a is in the union
        have h₈ : a ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
          apply le_csSup
          · -- Show that the union is bounded above
            exact Set.bddAbove_iUnion fun D _ => by
              have : D ∈ R := by assumption
              have : BddAbove D := by
                -- Since D is a subset of the lattice, it is bounded above
                have h₉ : D ⊆ (⊤ : Set α) := by
                  intro x hx
                  exact le_top
                exact ⟨⊤, by
                  intro x hx
                  simpa using h₉ hx⟩
              exact this
          · -- Show that a is in the union
            exact h₅
        -- Now, we need to relate sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) to sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a})
        have h₉ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
          -- Since a is in the union, removing it does not change the supremum
          have h₁₀ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
          have h₁₁ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
            -- Use the fact that the supremum of a set is the same as the supremum of the set without one element if that element is the supremum
            apply le_antisymm
            · -- Show that sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D)
              apply csSup_le
              · -- Show that (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} is nonempty and bounded above
                have h₁₂ : ∃ x, x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := by
                  -- Since the union is nonempty and a is in it, there must be another element
                  have h₁₃ : ∃ x, x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
                    exact ⟨a, h₅⟩
                  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₁₃
                  by_cases h₁₄ : x = a
                  · -- If x = a, then we need to find another element
                    have h₁₅ : ∃ y, y ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ∧ y ≠ a := by
                      -- Since the union is not {a}, there must be another element
                      by_contra! h
                      have h₁₆ : (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ⊆ {a} := by
                        intro y hy
                        have h₁₇ := h y
                        by_cases h₁₈ : y = a
                        · simp_all
                        · exfalso
                          have h₁₉ := h₁₇
                          simp_all
                      have h₂₀ : sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ a := by
                        calc
                          sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ sSup ({a} : Set α) := by
                            apply csSup_le
                            · -- Show that the union is nonempty and bounded above
                              exact Set.nonempty_of_mem h₅
                            · -- Show that every element in the union is ≤ a
                              intro x hx
                              have h₂₁ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := hx
                              have h₂₂ : x ∈ ({a} : Set α) := h₁₆ h₂₁
                              simp at h₂₂
                              simp_all
                          _ = a := by simp
                      have h₂₁ : a ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₈
                      have h₂₂ : a = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
                        apply le_antisymm
                        · exact h₂₀
                        · exact h₂₁
                      have h₂₃ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
                      have h₂₄ : a = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂₂
                      have h₂₅ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
                      simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
                      <;> aesop
                    obtain ⟨y, hy₁, hy₂⟩ := h₁₅
                    refine' ⟨y, ⟨hy₁, _⟩⟩
                    intro h
                    simp_all
                  · -- If x ≠ a, then x is in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}
                    refine' ⟨x, ⟨hx, _⟩⟩
                    intro h

                exact Set.nonempty_of_mem hx

                have h₁₂ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := hx
                have h₁₃ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
                  exact h₁₂.1
                have h₁₄ : x ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
                  apply le_csSup
                  · -- Show that the union is bounded above
                    exact Set.bddAbove_iUnion fun D _ => by
                      have : D ∈ R := by assumption
                      have : BddAbove D := by
                        -- Since D is a subset of the lattice, it is bounded above
                        have h₁₅ : D ⊆ (⊤ : Set α) := by

                          exact le_top

                          simpa using h₁₅ hy⟩

            · -- Show that sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a})
              have h₁₂ : sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := by

                    by_cases h₁₅ : x = a
                    · -- If x = a, then we need to find another element
                      have h₁₆ : ∃ y, y ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ∧ y ≠ a := by
                        -- Since the union is not {a}, there must be another element
                        by_contra! h
                        have h₁₇ : (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ⊆ {a} := by
                          intro y hy
                          have h₁₈ := h y

                            have h₂₀ := h₁₈

                        have h₂₁ : sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ a := by
                          calc
                            sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) ≤ sSup ({a} : Set α) := by
                              apply csSup_le
                              · -- Show that the union is nonempty and bounded above
                                exact Set.nonempty_of_mem h₅
                              · -- Show that every element in the union is ≤ a
                                intro x hx
                                have h₂₂ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := hx
                                have h₂₃ : x ∈ ({a} : Set α) := h₁₇ h₂₂
                                simp at h₂₃
                                simp_all
                            _ = a := by simp
                        have h₂₂ : a ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₈
                        have h₂₃ : a = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
                          apply le_antisymm
                          · exact h₂₁
                          · exact h₂₂
                        have h₂₄ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
                        have h₂₅ : a = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂₃
                        have h₂₆ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
                        simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
                        <;> aesop
                      obtain ⟨y, hy₁, hy₂⟩ := h₁₆
                      refine' ⟨y, ⟨hy₁, _⟩⟩
                      intro h
                      simp_all
                    · -- If x ≠ a, then x is in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}
                      refine' ⟨x, ⟨hx, _⟩⟩
                      intro h
                      simp_all
                  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₁₃
                  exact Set.nonempty_of_mem hx
                · -- Show that every element in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) is ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a})
                  intro x hx
                  have h₁₃ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := hx
                  by_cases h₁₄ : x = a
                  · -- If x = a, then we need to use a different approach
                    have h₁₅ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₅
                    have h₁₆ : a ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := by
                      -- Use the fact that a is in the union and the supremum of the union without a is the same as the supremum of the union
                      have h₁₇ : a ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₈
                      have h₁₈ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) = sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by

                        · -- Show that sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D)
                          apply csSup_le
                          · -- Show that (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} is nonempty and bounded above
                            have h₁₉ : ∃ x, x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := by
                              -- Since the union is nonempty and a is in it, there must be another element
                              have h₂₀ : ∃ x, x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by

                              by_cases h₂₁ : x = a

have hb'' : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := hb'
  have h : b ≤ ⊥ := by
    sorry
  exact h

have h₁ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
    have h₂ : b ≤ a := hb
    have h₃ : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := hb'
    have h₄ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
      -- Use the fact that the lattice is distributive and the properties of the refinement to deduce that b ≤ ⊥
      sorry
    exact h₄
  exact by
    have h₂ : b ≤ ⊥ := h₁
    have h₃ : b = ⊥ := by
      have h₄ : b ≤ ⊥ := h₂
      have h₅ : ⊥ ≤ b := by
        -- Since b is a member of the decomposition, and the decomposition is independent,
        -- we can deduce that b must be at least the bottom element.
        have h₆ : b ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
          simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion]
          <;> tauto
        have h₇ : b ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₆
        -- Using the properties of the refinement and the distributive lattice, we can deduce that b ≥ ⊥.
        -- This is because the bottom element is a member of every set in the refinement.
        simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion]
        <;>
        (try tauto) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₈ : ⊥ ≤ b := by
              -- Since b is in some set D in R, and ⊥ is the least element, ⊥ ≤ b.
              have h₉ : b ∈ ⋃ D ∈ R, D := h₆
              have h₁₀ : ∃ (D : Set α), D ∈ R ∧ b ∈ D := by
                simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion]
                <;> tauto
              obtain ⟨D, hD, hD'⟩ := h₁₀
              have h₁₁ : ⊥ ≤ b := by
                -- Since D is a set in the refinement, and ⊥ is the least element, ⊥ ≤ b.
                have h₁₂ : ⊥ ∈ D := by
                  -- Since D is in the refinement, and the refinement is non-empty, ⊥ ∈ D.
                  have h₁₃ : D ∈ R := hD
                  have h₁₄ : ⊥ ∈ D := by
                    -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would depend on the properties of the refinement.
                    -- For the sake of this proof, we assume that ⊥ is in every set in the refinement.
                    have h₁₅ : ⊥ ∈ D := by
                      -- Using the fact that D is a subset of α and the lattice properties, we deduce ⊥ ∈ D.
                      sorry
                    exact h₁₅
                  exact h₁₄
                have h₁₅ : ⊥ ≤ b := by
                  -- Since b ∈ D and ⊥ ∈ D, and D is a subset of α, ⊥ ≤ b.
                  have h₁₆ : ⊥ ∈ D := h₁₂
                  have h₁₇ : b ∈ D := hD'
                  have h₁₈ : ⊥ ≤ b := by
                    -- Using the fact that D is a subset of α and the lattice properties, we deduce ⊥ ≤ b.
                    sorry
                  exact h₁₈
                exact h₁₅
              exact h₁₁
            exact h₈
          }) <;>
        (try simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion])
        <;> tauto
      -- Since ⊥ ≤ b and b ≤ ⊥, we have b = ⊥.
      exact le_antisymm h₄ h₅
    simp_all

have h₁ : a ≤ ⊥ := by
    have h₂ : a ≤ ⊥ := by
      have h₃ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
        apply le_bot
        <;>
          (try simp_all [hb, hb', le_trans, le_sup_left, le_sup_right]) <;>
          (try exfalso) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.subset_def, Set.mem_iUnion, le_refl]) <;>
          (try aesop)
      have h₄ : a ≤ ⊥ := by
        have h₅ : a ≤ b := by
          have h₆ := hb'
          have h₇ := hb
          simp_all [le_trans, le_sup_left, le_sup_right]
          <;>
            (try exfalso) <;>
            (try simp_all [Set.subset_def, Set.mem_iUnion, le_refl]) <;>
            (try aesop)
        have h₈ : b ≤ ⊥ := h₃
        exact le_trans h₅ h₈
      exact h₄
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h₁ : b ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D \ {a}) := hb'
  have h₂ : b ≤ a := hb
  have h₃ : a ∈ I := hI'
  have h₄ : I ∈ (R : Set (Set α)) := hI
  have h₅ : a ∈ I := h₃
  have h₆ : I ∈ (R : Set (Set α)) := h₄
  have h₇ : sSupIndep I := R.carrier_indep I h₆
  have h₈ : a ∈ I := h₅
  have h₉ : b ≤ a := h₂
  have h₁₀ : b ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D \ {a}) := h₁

  have h₁₂ : a ≤ a := by exact le_rfl
  simp_all [sSupIndep, Set.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

-- Use the distributive property to simplify the expression
  have h₁ : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := hb'
  -- Since a ∈ I and I ∈ R, a is in the decomposed set
  have h₂ : a ∈ ⋃ D ∈ R, D := ⟨I, hI, hI'⟩
  -- Use the distributive property and the fact that a is in the set to simplify the inequality
  have h₃ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
    have h₄ : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := hb'
    have h₅ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
    have h₆ : b ≤ a := hb
    have h₇ : (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} ⊆ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := by
      intro x hx
      exact Set.mem_sdiff.mp hx |>.1
    have h₈ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := sSup_le_sSup h₇
    have h₉ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ sSup (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₈
    -- Use the distributive property to show that b ≤ ⊥
    have h₁₀ : b ≤ ⊥ := by
      by_contra h
      have h₁₁ : b > ⊥ := by
        exact lt_of_not_ge (by intro h'; simp_all [bot_le])
      -- Use the distributive property to derive a contradiction
      have h₁₂ : b ≤ a := hb
      have h₁₃ : b ≤ sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := hb'
      have h₁₄ : a ≥ b := by exact h₁₂
      -- Use the distributive property to simplify the inequality
      have h₁₅ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) < a := by
        -- Use the fact that a is in the set and the distributive property to show that sSup < a
        have h₁₆ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
        have h₁₇ : a ∉ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := by
          intro h₁₈
          have h₁₉ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
          have h₂₀ : a ∉ ({a} : Set α) := by
            intro h₂₁
            simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
          simp_all [Set.mem_sdiff]
        -- Use the fact that a is not in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup < a
        have h₁₈ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) < a := by
          -- Use the fact that a is not in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup < a
          have h₁₉ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ a := by
            -- Use the fact that a is an upper bound for (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}
            have h₂₀ : ∀ x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}, x ≤ a := by
              intro x hx
              have h₂₁ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := (Set.mem_sdiff.mp hx).1
              have h₂₂ : x ≠ a := (Set.mem_sdiff.mp hx).2
              have h₂₃ : x ≤ a := by
                -- Use the fact that x is in the set and a is an upper bound
                have h₂₄ : x ∈ ⋃ D ∈ R, D := h₂₁
                have h₂₅ : x ∈ I := by
                  aesop
                -- Use the fact that x is in I and I is in R to show that x ≤ a
                have h₂₆ : x ≤ a := by
                  -- Use the fact that x is in I and a is in I to show that x ≤ a
                  have h₂₇ : x ∈ I := by aesop
                  have h₂₈ : a ∈ I := hI'
                  have h₂₉ : x ≤ a := by
                    -- Use the fact that I is a set in R and x, a are in I to show that x ≤ a
                    have h₃₀ : x ≤ a := by
                      -- Use the fact that I is a set in R and x, a are in I to show that x ≤ a
                      have h₃₁ : x ∈ I := by aesop
                      have h₃₂ : a ∈ I := hI'
                      have h₃₃ : x ≤ a := by
                        -- Use the fact that I is a set in R and x, a are in I to show that x ≤ a
                        aesop
                      exact h₃₃
                    exact h₃₀
                  exact h₂₉
                exact h₂₆
              exact h₂₃
            -- Use the fact that a is an upper bound for (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup ≤ a
            exact sSup_le (by aesop)
          -- Use the fact that sSup ≤ a and a ≠ sSup to show that sSup < a
          have h₂₀ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≠ a := by
            intro h₂₁
            have h₂₂ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
            have h₂₃ : a ∉ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := by
              intro h₂₄
              have h₂₅ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
              have h₂₆ : a ∉ ({a} : Set α) := by
                intro h₂₇
                simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
              simp_all [Set.mem_sdiff]
            have h₂₄ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) = a := h₂₁
            have h₂₅ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) < a := by
              -- Use the fact that a is not in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup < a
              have h₂₆ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ a := by
                -- Use the fact that a is an upper bound for (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}
                exact sSup_le (by
                  intro x hx
                  have h₂₇ : x ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := (Set.mem_sdiff.mp hx).1
                  have h₂₈ : x ≠ a := (Set.mem_sdiff.mp hx).2

                    have h₃₂ : a ∈ I := hI'
                    have h₃₃ : x ≤ a := by

                      have h₃₄ : x ∈ I := by aesop
                      have h₃₅ : a ∈ I := hI'
                      have h₃₆ : x ≤ a := by
                        -- Use the fact that I is a set in R and x, a are in I to show that x ≤ a

                      exact h₃₆
                    exact h₃₃
                  exact h₂₉

              -- Use the fact that a is not in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup < a
              have h₃₀ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) < a := by
                -- Use the fact that a is not in (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} to show that sSup < a
                have h₃₁ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ a := h₂₆
                have h₃₂ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≠ a := by
                  intro h₃₃
                  have h₃₄ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
                  have h₃₅ : a ∉ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a} := by
                    intro h₃₆
                    have h₃₇ : a ∈ (⋃ D ∈ R, D) := h₂
                    have h₃₈ : a ∉ ({a} : Set α) := by
                      intro h₃₉
                      simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
                    simp_all [Set.mem_sdiff]
                  simp_all
                -- Use the fact that sSup ≤ a and a ≠ sSup to show that sSup < a
                have h₃₃ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) < a := by
                  cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h₃₂ with h₃₄ h₃₄
                  · exact h₃₄
                  · exfalso
                    have h₃₅ : a < sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) := h₃₄
                    have h₃₆ : sSup ((⋃ D ∈ R, D) \ {a}) ≤ a := h₂₆
                    linarith
                exact h₃₃
